title: Osisko’s Gaspé revamp ties history to copper’s surge
summary: For Osisko Metals (TSX: OM) chief operating officer Jeff Hussey, life has a way of repeating itself. Three decades ago, Hussey was hired by Toronto-based mining giant Noranda to work as a geologist at the Gaspé Copper mine in Murdochville, Quebec, about 825 km northeast of Montreal. It was there that he met the man who would go on to become his boss at Osisko, Robert Wares. Today, 27 years after low metal prices shut Gaspé, both men are part of the team leading Osisko’s multi-billion-dollar drive to reopen the mine – which holds the biggest undeveloped copper resource east of the Mississippi – amid a global boom that has analysts predicting supply shortfalls for years to come. “For both of us it’s kind of full circle,” Hussey told The Northern Miner in an interview last month. “Gas...
